panels.title: CPU load — %core (busy = work it does; steal = host throttling). Watch step-ups against Deploys.
panels.description: filmowo is shared-cpu-1x, so 100 = one full core. 'busy' is the work it does (user+system+io); 'steal' is CPU the host withheld once demand crossed the shared-CPU credit — near-zero at rest, it appears when a recommendation build saturates the core. A step-up lining up with a purple Deploy marker means that release added load. Fly platform metric, so only the filmowo app is included.

panels.title: CPU credit balance (alarm < 500)
panels.description: shared-cpu-1x credit bucket. Sustained CPU above the earn rate (a long recommendation build) drains it; when it hits zero Fly throttles filmowo hard (see the throttle + steal panels). A steady decline is the early warning.

panels.title: CPU throttled (rate, fraction of wall-clock)
panels.description: Fraction of wall-clock the Fly host throttled filmowo's CPU because the shared-CPU credit ran out. Non-zero here is the direct cause of request-latency spikes during a recommendation build.

panels.title: CPU steal %
panels.description: Share of filmowo's CPU time the host withheld (steal ÷ total). Rises when the shared core is contended — the build competing with request serving on one core.

panels.title: Memory available % (alarm < 8%)
panels.description: Free RAM as a fraction of total on the filmowo machine (512MB). node + node:sqlite + Litestream + the build worker share it; a dip toward the floor risks OOM.

panels.title: HTTP latency (s)
panels.description: Request latency percentiles from the Fly proxy's response-time histogram for filmowo. p99 spikes here are the user-visible symptom of a CPU-throttled build window.

panels.title: Instance up
panels.description: 1 = the filmowo machine is up. A drop to 0 (or a gap) is a restart — correlate with a Deploy marker, an OOM, or a health-check kill during a build.

panels.title: Event-loop lag — main thread (p99 / mean / max)
panels.description: How long the main thread's event loop is delayed past schedule — the direct measure of request responsiveness on the single shared core. Spikes line up with a recommendation build contending for the core; the request-priority scheduler + worker nice keep it low while a search is in flight. prom-client default metric via /metrics (app-metrics-live, ~15s fresh) — NOT available on the laggy fly-prometheus.

panels.title: HTTP requests by route (per min)
panels.description: Request rate per bucketed route (filmowo_http_requests_total). /api/* routes by exact path; hashed assets and the SPA shell bucket to static / app-shell so the series set stays bounded.

panels.title: HTTP latency p99 by route (s)
panels.description: 99th-percentile handler wall-clock per route, from the filmowo_http_request_duration_seconds histogram. /api/search here is the number the scheduler work targets — read it against the event-loop lag panel above.

panels.title: Search in flight — the build worker parks while > 0
panels.description: filmowo_search_inflight: latency-sensitive search requests being served right now. While > 0 the recommendation build parks the shared CPU (Atomics.wait) so the search gets the core; back to 0 and the build resumes. A step to 1+ that coincides with an event-loop-lag dip is the request-priority scheduler working.

panels.title: Event-loop stalls (per min)
panels.description: Rate of event-loop stalls past the 250ms perf threshold (filmowo_event_loop_stalls_total) — the count the pacing + scheduler work drove down ~10×. A non-zero rate is a build (or other synchronous work) blocking the loop.

panels.title: DB — calls/s and avg call time (ms)
panels.description: Main-thread node:sqlite pressure. calls/s = rate(filmowo_db_calls_total); avg ms = rate(seconds)/rate(calls)×1000 (RIGHT axis). node:sqlite is synchronous, so DB time IS event-loop time — a rising average is queries getting slower under load.

panels.title: Node memory — heap used / RSS
panels.description: Process memory of the filmowo Node server: V8 heap used and total resident set (filmowo_nodejs_heap_size_used_bytes / filmowo_process_resident_memory_bytes). Read against the 512MB machine — headroom shared by node + node:sqlite + Litestream + the build worker (see the Fly 'Memory available %' panel above).
